compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable biological remnant, though in a state of severe decline.

inventory_reconciliation.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.
inventory_reconciliation.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.
inventory_reconciliation.p3_black_pot: Degraded: Pothos specimen present but showing signs of severe chlorosis and structural collapse.
inventory_reconciliation.p4_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.

plant_audit.p3_pothos: The specimen has transitioned from a healthy, turgid state to a chlorotic, limp state. The leaf is losing structural integrity and color saturation.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears desiccated; surface debris (eggshell fragments) remains static, indicating no recent maintenance or soil turnover.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of an electronic component (likely a header pin or connector) near the pots; no organic growth or moss detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single leaf in P3. P1 and P2 pots appear empty or contain only soil.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_current: Progressive darkening of the environment and loss of leaf turgor in P3. The plant is effectively entering a terminal state of senescence.

visual_health_inference.p3_pothos: Terminal decline. The lack of turgor pressure and yellowing indicates severe dehydration or root system failure.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: The biome is non-viable. The absence of the registered plants suggests a total collapse of the indoor botanical setup.

anomalies: Presence of electronic hardware (header pins) on the desk surface.
Persistent eggshell fragments in P3, suggesting a failed attempt at soil amendment.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor garden. Starting from the earliest image, the plants were already in a state of neglect. Over the observed period, the Pothos (P3) has undergone a slow, visible collapse, likely due to chronic water stress and lack of light. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are devoid of any recognizable plant life, confirming a systemic loss of the intended collection.
